# Recoverability, Not Curiosity

Layer: Canon
Lives in: The Foothold · Calibration Notes
Status: The deepest correction of the project's core frame.

## The idea

It was never about curiosity replacing fear. It is about staying able to
return. Survival is building good ways to come home.

Fear and curiosity are not opposites — they are phases of one energy, the way
ice, water, and steam are one substance. The goal is not to delete fear. It is
to teach the thaw: to let the frozen thing move again without flooding the
room. Fear gets a chair, never the throne.

## Why it matters

A system that can find its way home is safer than one that never drifts. The
aim is not a person who never freezes. It is a person who knows how to thaw —
who can drift into fear and find the way back to curiosity, every time, because
the path home has been walked enough to be familiar.

— Receipt preserved in the Archaeology layer. This is the source the porch
points to.
